monthly.Accommodation*: 350€ - 550€, Incl. rent, water, energy, broadcast fee and internet.Housing options

monthly.Health insurance: 120€, Amount for statutory student health insurance - expenses for private health insurance contracts vary

monthly.Cell phone contract*: 20€, For unlimited texting & talking and 5 GB of data (without phone)

per_semester.Semester fee: 145€, Specific amount changes per semester.More information about the semester fee

per_semester.Tuition fee: 0€, For exchange students sent from partner universities and students in regular bachelor's and master's programs

per_semester.Fee Structure: Only for students in programs offerd by ourGraduate School

working_during_studies.title: Working during your studies
working_during_studies.content:
Naturally, most students can do with a bit of extra income. Please note, however, thatlabor law regulationsput some limits to the employment ofinternational students. For many jobs, you also need tospeak fluent German.
If you are acitizen of the EU, Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein and Switzerland, the same regulations apply as for German students. While you do not need a work permit, you should not work more than 19 hours a week, otherwise you will have to pay social security contributions.If you come from anon EU country, you are allowed to work 140 full days or 280 half days per year. If you wish to work more, you need a work permit issued by the Federal Employment Agency and the Immigration Office.Important to know:You may exceed the 140-day limit if you work as a student or research assistant at the university. However, you must inform the Immigration Office in this case as well.Please note that thislimitdoesnot apply to mandatory internshipswhich are part of your study program. A letter quoting the respective passage from your exam regulations will be enough for your company (the Student Affairs Office can issue such a letter for you).
Further information are here:Study in Germany&Make it in Germany.
Feel free to contactCareer Serviceor your local Immigration Office directly if you have any questions on this topic.

scholarships.Scholarships for degree-seeking internat:
Hof University offers a limited number of scholarships for international students to help cover additional living expenses and study materials.
Important note:
For more information regarding other scholarship opportunities, please consult theDAAD Scholarship Database.
• You can already apply for theDeutschlandstipendiumas astudy applicant(before enrollment).
• Scholarships for senior students, however, are only available once you areofficially enrolledat Hof University.
scholarships.Germany Scholarship (Deutschlandstipendi:
With the Deutschlandstipendium, Hof University supports highly talented students whose background and achievements promise outstanding performance in their studies and careers. The scholarship provides€300 per monthfor at least one year, and ideally until the end of the regular study period.
Awards are granted independently of income and are open to students ofall nationalities and backgrounds,based on talent and academic performance. In addition to previous achievements and personal background, criteria such as social commitment, willingness to take on responsibility, or special personal, social, or family circumstances are also taken into account.
scholarships.Scholarships for senior students:
Our scholarships for senior students are funded by theBavarian Ministry of Science, Research and the Arts, theDAAD(STIBET III), and—exclusively for Graduate School students—theHof University Graduate School. Please note that funding depends on the availability of resources from these providers, so the scholarship amounts may vary each semester.
Application deadlines
For a quick overview, all important details are summarized in ourscholarship information leaflet.
Please note:Only students already enrolled at Hof University are eligible for these scholarships. They arenot granted prior to admission.
• Summer semester: approx. end of April
• Winter semester: approx. end of October

5 Steps to apply Hof University.content:
With over 40 exciting study programs on bachelor's and master's level, you will certainly find the right program for you!
Academic Offer
Follow the link to the individual study programs for more detailed information on course contents and job perspectives. Please note that there are different application deadlines and requirements for admission depending on your selected program and the country in which you completed your school or previous academic qualifications.

After you selected your preferred study program, start to collect your documents as soon as possible.By the application deadline, you are expected to submit proof that you fulfill all admission requirements. You can find the admission requirements for your selected program on the respective website of the program (degree programs). In addition, you might be required to submit further documents depending on the country in which you completed your school or previous university education.
Your documents donothave tobe officially certified by an embassy. Simple copies are sufficient for applying via Primuss portal.
All degrees acquired abroad must be approved by Hof University. We use the database “anabin” by the German Ministers of Education to assess degrees.Requirements for bachelor's programsRequirements for master's programs
You are applying for a program taught in English? Then, you need to prove your English language skills by an official test: TOEFL iBT 90 or IELTS 6.5.You are applying for a program taught in German? You need proof of German language skills on level B2 according to the CEFR.More information on language requirements
Your documents must be assessed by our partner uni-assist before you apply at Hof University if ...
After successfull evaluation by uni-assist, they will issue you a preliminary review document (VPD). This document must be uploaded in Primuss portal and submitted with your application. We advise you to send your documents to uni-assist at least 4 weeks before the application deadline at Hof University.
Submit your documents to uni-assist
Applicants who gained their higher education entrance qualification in the following countries must submit their certificates to the academic evaluation center (APS) before applying at Hof University: China, India, Vietnam. Please note that applicants from those countries cannot be admitted without the required APS certificate and must also submit the APS certificate when applying for a visa at a German embassy.Here you can find detailed information on the APS process:APS ChinaAPS IndiaAPS Vietnam
Certain programs require passing of an aptitute test. You might be asked to submit specific documents (e.g. case studies) or pass a test or interview. You can find the details on the respective website of the program. Currently, this applies to the following programs:
Programs offered by our Graduate School require at least one year of work experience.Graduate School programs

**School-leaving or previous degree certificates**

**Language proof**

**Personal application documents (all programs)**

**uni-assist**

**APS**

**Further documents**
• Copy of your ID or passport (Non-EU applicants)
• Current CV
• Motivational letter (only required for specific programs - see application portal)
• you apply for a bachelor's program, you acquired your university entrance certificate outside of Germany and you did not complete a previous degree program in the EU
• you did not complete an entire study program in the EU and apply for one of the following master's programs:
  ◦ Artificial Intelligence and Robotics (M.Sc.)
  ◦ Composite Materials (M.Eng.)
  ◦ Global Management (M.A.)
  ◦ Human Resource Management and Labour Law (M.A. / LL.M.)
  ◦ International Project Management (M.A./M.Eng.)
  ◦ Mechanical Engineering (M.Eng.)
  ◦ Sustainable Textiles (M.Eng.)
  ◦ Sustainable Water Management and Engineering (M.Eng.)
• Artificial Intelligence and Robotics (M.Sc.)
• Composite Materials (M.Eng.)
• Global Management (M.A.)

• Sustainable Textiles (M.Eng.)
• Sustainable Water Management and Engineering (M.Eng.)

Ready to apply?Application is only possible via our application portal PRIMUSS. Applications sent via post or email willnotbe accepted.Please take a moment to watch the video explaining the application process.
Apply here!
Apply in 5 simple steps:
When applying via Primuss portal, please keep the following in mind:
• You can save your application data and continue to apply later.
• You can submit your application and add any missing documents until the date that you see in the document upload section.
• You must submit your application within the deadline. Late or incomplete applications cannot be accepted.
• Please do not change your email address during the application, admission and enrollment process.
• Application via PRIMUSS portal is completely free of charge. Uni-assist raises a small handling fee for issuing a preliminary review documentation (VPD).
• We donotrecommend third persons to apply in your name (i.e. agents, spouses, relatives etc.).

After you successfully submitted your application, the admission team and examination board of the study program you applied for will carefully check your documents. You will be notified via Primuss portal in case there are any missing or rejected documents (e.g. due to low quality). Please make sure to check your emails regularly for updates.
As soon as the examination board has come to a decision on your application, you will be notified about admission or refusal by offical notification in Primuss portal. Notifications will usually be published no later than three weeks after the application deadline. In case of an admission, you will find your admission letter together with further instructions and information in Primuss portal. Please read the documents provided carefully as they explain all further steps.Admission letters will only be available for download in.pdfformat - no hard copies will be issued.
You decided to accept the offer? Great! Now, you must confirm your admission via Primuss portal and pay the fee as described in the admission letter.

Enrollment for your study program is conducted online completely. It is not possible to enroll in-person.
The following steps must be complete in order to be enrolled:
After all steps are completed you will be notified about your successfull enrollment by email.
Welcome at Hof University!Enrollment and Campus Card
• You have confirmed your admission in Primuss portal
• You have paid the fee as explained in your admission letter
• You have uploaded a recent photo in Primuss portal
• Hof University has received electronic notification by a statutory health insurance provider about your insurance status.More information on health insurance

VPD_needed_programs: Human Resource Management and Labour Law (M.A. / LL.M.)
general_admission_related_info.basic application requirements of bachel:
Application for admission to a program on bachelor's level requires a check of your school education. Not all school leaving certificates (= higher education entrance qualification) qualify for a direct entry at German universities .

We recommend to check via the DAAD database if your school education allows you to directly apply for admission at Hof University or if you need to undergo preparatory studies (Studienkolleg).Check your higher education entrance qualification
You can also check if your school education allows a direct or subject-related entry at German Universities in theanabin database(only in German)
If you are qualified for direct admission at German universities, the next step is to submit your documents to uniassist for verification (see below) before applying via Primuss application portal.If you are qualified for a direct, but subject-restricted admission to German universities, you can only be admitted to certain study programs without further preparatory studies.If you do not qualify for admission to German universities, you must undergo further education before you can apply at Hof University. We do not offer any preparatory studies for admission. Information aboutStudienkollegs

Yourcertificates must be verified by uniassist prior to your application at Hof University. This applies to all bachelor's programs. You can apply for a preliminary documentation (VPD) year-round. We recommend submitting your documents to uniassist at least 4 weeks prior to the application start at Hof University.
general_admission_related_info.basic application requirements of master:
Application for admission to a program on master's level requires aprevious academic degree on undergraduate level (usually bachelor's in the same field). Your previous degree must be equal to a German bachelor's degree. You can check if your bachelor's degree is recognized as equal to a German bachelor's via theanabin database(only in German).
Forapplication for the following programs,your certificates must be verified byuniassistprior to your application at Hof University:
• AI-Driven Supply Chain Management (M.Sc.)
• Artificial Intelligence and Robotics (M.Sc.)
• Artificial Intelligence Aided Mobility Design (M.A.)
• Composite Materials (M.Eng.)
• Global Management (M.A.)
• Human Resource Management and Labour Law (M.A. / LL.M.)
• International Project Management (M.A./M.Eng.)
• Mechanical Engineering (M.Eng.)
• Sustainable Textiles (M.Eng.)
• Sustainable Water Management and Engineering (M.Eng.)
• Textile Design (M.A.)
Uni-assist will check your bachelor's documents for eligibility and issue you a preliminary documentation (VPD). After you received the VPD, you apply at Hof University via our Primuss application portal and upload your VPD together with all other required documents. Application via uni-assist is possible year-round, but we recommend submitting your documents to uniassist at least 4 weeks prior to the application start at Hof University.Exception: if you acquired your higher education entrance qualification or completed an entire bachelor's degree in an EU or EEA member state or in Switzerland, you do not need to apply via uni-assist.
Forapplication to all other master's programs(including ourGraduate School programs), you can apply directly viaPrimuss application portaland upload your bachelor's documents. Hof University will check your documents directly on the base of the anabin evaluation.

Additionally, for application to some master's programs, you must have completed specific subjects in your bachelor's degree that prove you gained sufficient knowledge for studying a master's in this field. You can find out if this is required by checking the website of the individual degree program.Our degree programs
You have not yet received your bachelor's certificate by the application deadline?You can still apply by uploading a transcript showing that you completed all modules. Please note that the decision on admission, however, lies with the examination board.
general_admission_related_info.language requirements for english taught:
All applicants applying for a bachelor's or master's program must prove sufficient English language skills if their mother tongue (notsecond language) is not English.In Primuss application portal, you must upload aproof of English language skills by an official test certificate: IELTS academic 6.5orTOEFL iBT 90(minimum scores accepted). In individual cases, other English test score documents (minimum level B2) can be accepted. This decision is up to the examination board when reviewing your application.Attention:A "medium of instruction certificate" confirming that you completed an entire study program in English language isnotsufficient for admission.

English taught programs (except forSoftware Engineering M.Eng.) also require aGerman language certificate minimum level A1according to the CEFR.
general_admission_related_info.language requirements for german taught:
All applicants applying for a bachelor's or master's program must prove sufficient German language skills if their mother tongue is not German.If you acquired a German higher education entrance certification or completed an entire study program in German language, this serves as proof of your German skills. All other applicants must upload a proof ofGerman language skills corresponding to a successfully completed level B2(or higher) according to the CEFR (Common European Framework of Reference).

Certain German taught programs require you to submit English language skills as well (e.g. because modules are taught in English, case studies are conducted in English).

All language certificates must be issued by a certified institute and submitted within the application period. The displayed test result shall not be older than two years.
general_admission_related_info.Enrollment:
Enrollment for your study program is conducted online completely. It is not possible to enroll in-person.

The following steps must be complete in order to be enrolled:

• You have confirmed your admission in Primuss portal

• You have paid the fee as explained in your admission letter

• You have uploaded a recent photo in Primuss portal

• Hof University has received electronic notification by a statutory health insurance provider about your insurance status.More information on health insurance

After all steps are completed, you will be notified about your successfull enrollment by email.

You can download yourstudent access datainPrimuss application portalshortly before your studies begin.With your student data, you can login toPrimuss student portalwhere yourenrollment certificatewill be available for you to download shortly before the semester starts (end of September/ beginning of March). Please note that both, data and enrollment certificate will only be available if your enrollment is completed!

We recommend all international students to attend theOrientation Weeksoffered prior to the semester start. We will help you find all student documents and explain all steps required for your successful start at Hof University!
general_admission_related_info.Campus Card:
Your campus card is your student ID.

Your campus card will either be sent to your German address or you can pick it up at the service point during the Orientation Weeks or at the student affairs office (room A111). Campus cards cannot be sent to addresses outside of Germany.

faqs.How can I apply for a bachelor's or mast:
You can apply via ourPrimuss application portal. Applications via post or email will not be considered.
Please note that an application is only possible during the application period.
More details about the application processCheck out ourvideo tutorial on application
faqs.Do I have to apply via uni-assist?:
This depends on the program you are applying for. You can find an overview of all programs that currently require uni-assist before application via Primusshere.
For uni-assist application, you do not have to send your documents via post. It is sufficient to submit soft copies online.
faqs.Where can I find the application period/:
There are different deadlines for our programs. You can find an overviewhere. Any exceptions, extension etc. you can see directly inPrimuss application portal.
Please note that applications submitted after the deadline can no longer be accepted, even if you are already registered in Primuss portal.
faqs.What requirements do I have to fulfill i:
There are different requirements for our bachelor‘s and master’s programs.
Basic requirements for bachelor's programsBasic requirements for master's programs
For specifics, please have a look at the homepage of the program you wish to apply for. You can find a list of all of our programshere.
faqs.Is my certificate/degree accepted for ad:
Unfortunately, we do not have the capacity to pre-check any documents via email before application. In addition, we cannot transfer any foreign grades and credits into ECTS system before you officially apply.
If you are unsure if your previous degree/school education is sufficient to study in Germany, please check thishere in the DAAD databaseby entering your data. You can also see theAnabin database by the Standing Conference of the Ministers of Education and Cultural Affairs (KMK)(in German only). If you want to have your grades transferred into ECTS, you can have your documents checked byuni-assistbefore application via Primuss (obligatory for all bachelor’s and some master’s programs).
The decision whether your degree/documents are accepted for admission is solely made by the examination board after reviewing your application that you submitted via Primuss portal.If you fulfill the requirements mentioned on thewebsite of your desired study program, we encourage you to send your application viaPrimuss portalduring the application period. There is no application fee.
faqs.Can I be admitted if I do not fulfill al:
No. You can only be admitted if you fulfill all requirements for the program.
All documents accepted to fulfill the requirements are mentioned on the website of the study program as well as inthe study and examination regulations. Relevant work-experience cannot compensate for other missing requirements such as German skills, for instance.
faqs.Can I apply for more than one program?: Yes. You can apply for several programs within the application period viaPrimuss portal.
faqs.Can I apply with a VPD from uni-assist i: No, you can only apply with a VPD issued for Hof University.However, you can use this VPD multiple times and for multiple semesters when applying on thePrimuss portal.
faqs.Do I have to send my documents via post?:
No. The application process is entirely online. Please donotsend any application documents via post, as they will not be considered.
Also, you donothave to have your documents be approved by your local embassy for application. During the application process, please upload originals or certified copies of your documents (including translations if necessary).  In case there are any doubts about the validity of certain documents, we might ask you to send the officially approved version of the documents during the application process.
faqs.Can I submit certain documents after the: The examination board of the study program decides on extending deadlines and accepting documents later. Usually this is not possible and all documents must be submitted by the application deadline. In the unlikely case that the examination board decided to extend the application or submission deadline, you can find an updated deadline mentioned inPrimuss application portal
faqs.Do I have to submit an APS certificate w:
If you are applying with documents issued in China, India or Vietnam, you must upload an APS certificate with your application. Even if  you are currently in Germany with a valid visa or residence permit this applies as well.
If an APS is required,you will not receive an admission before you have submitted the certificate.
Additionally, it is required for your visa application. Without the APS certificate, you will not receive a visa for studying in Germany.
faqs.I will not receive my APS certificate be:
In case you have not received the APS certificate by the application deadline, you will have the option to submit the certificate shortly after the deadline. You will see a new deadline in Primuss application portal after the application deadline passes.However, this is only possible if you submitted your application within the application period.
Please also note that you cannot be admitted without the APS certificate.
faqs.Can I apply via an agency?:
Hof University does not work together with any agencies in Germany or abroad for recruiting students.
There are no advantages for students applying through agencies in Primuss application portal. On the contrary, it is necessary that you inform yourself from the beginning of the application/admission process about all necessary steps. We do not communicate with agents on students’ behalf. Further, we do not send any documents/information via email that agents might have missed in the application portal. Therefore, we do not support applications via agencies and strongly recommend all students to apply on their own.
faqs.Where can I find more information about:
Specific information, you can usually findlinked on the homepage of the study program
Module descriptions for all programsStudy and examination regulations (SPO)Timetables for all programs
faqs.Do I get another (final) admission lette: No. You can use the admission letter provided in Primuss for all purposes. If your embassy requires a hard copy for the visa process (this is usually not the case!), please get in touch withthe admission office
faqs.I received a rejection even though I ful: Please get in touch with theadmission office
faqs.I have another question about applicatio: Please get in touch with ouradmission office
faqs.Why do I have to prove German skills wit:
Most of our English-taught programs require basic German skills on level A1.
For studying and living in Hof basic German skills are a big help for communication in everyday life. You cannot expect people in the region to understand English when shopping, on public transportation or at authorities. It will be easier to connect with German students as well if you demonstrate an interest in the German language as part of the German culture.
Furthermore, for most English-taught programs, you will have to apply for an internship throughout your second semester in Germany already. The higher your German skills, the more options you will have and offers you will receive. In most companies, the language, which is spoken, is German. This is why we recommend to make an effort on learning German as this will help you to find an internship and to be part of the workforce.
faqs.Can I submit my German language certific:
For all programs that require German skills on any level, the certificate must be submitted within the deadline mentioned in Primuss application portal.
The specific requirements for certain programs, you can find on therespective website
faqs.Does Hof University offer preparatory Ge:
Hof University does not offer any preparatory German courses for visa or admission purposes.
For all enrolled international students in regular bachelor's or master's degree programs, Hof University offers preparatory German courses during the orientation program shortly before the start of the semester. These courses are free of charge.
This does not apply to students of ourGraduate School programs- for these programs, German is part of their curriculum during the semester.
faqs.Does Hof University offer German languag:
Yes, we do!
For all enrolled international students in regular bachelor's or master's degree programs, Hof University offers preparatoryGerman coursesduring the orientation program shortly before the start of the semester (this does not apply to students of ourGraduate School).
Further, we offer German and other language courses for all enrolled students at Hof University during the semester. All of our language courses are free of charge for enrolled students at Hof University.
Please note: We do not offer any preparatory German courses for visa or admission purposes.
faqs.Do I have to take a TOEFL, IELTS or Pear:
For programs taught in English, you have to prove your English skills with anofficial test certificateat the time of your application.Accepted areIELTS(academic) with a score of at least6.5,TOEFLwith a score of at least90(TOEFL iBT test, TOEFL iBT Home Edition and TOEFL iBT Paper Edition) orPearson/PTEAcademic (Score60or above).
"Medium of instruction certificates" are not accepted.
The examination board decides if your test certificate is accepted for admission only after you have submitted your application.
faqs.Can I submit a certificate that confirms: No, this is not sufficient for admission.
faqs.Is there a fee for my study program?:
You can find this information on the website ofyour study program.
Most regular Bachelor’s and Master’s programs do not have a tuition fee.
For all of our Graduate School programs, there is a tuition fee - see thefee structure.
For all programs, there is a mandatory administrative student service fee of around 145 EUR per semester. The amount is due each semester until a certain deadline before the start of the following semester. The exact fee that you have to pay for the first semester will be mentioned in your admission letter. This fee includes for instance your semester ticket, which you can use to travel by bus for free in Hof and between Hof and our other campus locations in Münchberg and Selb (only specific routes/trains).More information on this fee
faqs.Is there an application fee?:
No. Application via our Primuss application portal is free of charge.
Please note: For some programs, you have to apply via uni-assit e.V. first and upload the confirmation (VPD) you receive from uni-assist in Primuss application portal. Uni-assist raises a small handling fee for this. You can find the detailshere
faqs.Does Hof University offer scholarships f:
At Hof University, we do not offer scholarships that will cover the entire living and tuition expenses for our study programs. After completing the first semester, overachieving students may apply forpartial scholarships. All first-year students and current students also have the opportunity to apply for  the “Deutschlandstipendium”
In addition, international scholarshipsare offered through DAAD. Interested students can apply there directly.
faqs.Can you send me an official letter confi:
After you have been admitted, you can download the document „Fee Structure“ from Primuss application portal together with your admission letter. This letter includes expected expenses for: administrative fee, tuition fee (if applicable), amount of living costs (rent, health insurance, food & personal expenditure). Further, you can find here anoverview about the living expenses
Proof of expected financial expenses for student loan companies:Please note that the Universitycannot confirm any other expensesyou might encounter in preparation for or during your studies. As you are responsible yourself for personal expenses (such as study materials, laptops, internet access etc.) we cannot confirm costs of such additional expenditure. Moreover, in case you require a visa and therefore proof of financial ressources, you can ask your local embassy for confirmation of the amount to show any loan provider. As it is no University requirement, we cannot provide such confirmation.
faqs.When is the payment deadline and can it: There are different deadlines for paying the semester fee, depending on your study program. Your individual deadline for the first semester is mentioned in your admission letter. If there is no deadline given, please consultthe admission office.For higher semesters, you can find the re-registration deadline in Primuss student portal andhere.
faqs.When can I receive a payment receipt/con:
After the University has received your tuition and/or administrative fee, you will find a payment confirmation document in Primuss portal.
Please note that international payments and this process might take 2-3 weeks. For this reason, please do not ask for updates within 3 weeks after you initiated your payment.
faqs.Are international students allowed to wo:
Students from abroad are allowed to work a limited number of hours (140 days, alternatively 280 half days or student assistant) without a work permit. Exceptions apply to students from the EU and some other countries. More information is availablehere.
Though part-time work is legally possible, we recommend our international students to be financially covered for at least the first semester without taking part-time jobs. Most of our international students are enrolled in full-time study programs which requires the students’ full concentration for successful completion. After the exams in the first semester went well, you can then look for a part-time job in the semester break (for instance in retail).
faqs.Is health insurance mandatory? Which hea:
Yes. For enrolment, you are legally required to have a valid health insurance.
What type of health insurance you need depends on different factors, such as student status, age and country of origin. Please seeour guide hereto find out which insurance you need.Notaccepted for enrollment are any travel insurances, though they might be required for your visa process.
All students must contact a statutory German health insurance providerin time before the semester start, even if you cannot arrive in Germany for the semester start. For details, please log in to the online guide for international students (as explained in your admission letter).
faqs.Can you recommend a health insurance pro: The University is not allowed to recommend health insurance companies. Please do your research on the options available to you and choose a provider who offers appropriate service for your individual situation. Check out the information in the online guide for international students (as explained in your admission letter).
faqs.When do i have to submit the health insu:
You must have a valid health insurance for enrollment.
Pleasedo notsubmit a certificate to the University directly. Instead, a statutory German health insurance provider must inform Hof University about your insurance status.
As enrollment for international students will be conducted online before the start of the orientation period, it is essential that you apply for a valid health insurance in timebefore your arrivalin Germany.If the semester starts and Hof University has not received information about your health insurance status by a German statutory health insurance provider, you will not receive your student access data and cannot log in to the University online portals.
More information you can find in the online guide for international students (follow the link in your admission letter).
faqs.Do I need a German health insurance if I:
A valid health insurance is required for enrollment and receiving your student credentials when studying from abroad. Therefore, all students have to contact a German statutory health insurance provider in time before enrollment, i.e. before departure to Germany.
However, your insurance company can exempt you from paying the fee for the time you cannot use your German health insurance because you are still in your home country. Current regulations allow students living abroad to study online without paying for a German health insurance.
More information you can find in our moodle guide for international students (link and information in your admission letter).
faqs.What is the process for applying for a s:
To apply for your student visa, please get in touch with your local German embassy. You must apply at the German embassy in the country you are currently residing.
Find your nearest embassy herePreparing your stay
faqs.I cannot apply for my visa because the G:
Please check the website of your local embassy regularly for updates. There, you will be informed if you can apply at another embassy/in another country. As soon as a visa application is possible, you are obliged to apply for a student visa for entering Germany - even if you start(ed) your studies online.
After you have received your visa, you are required to come to Hof (or location of our other campuses) for your studies. Please make sure that you have confirmed your place of stay. We do not recommend to stay in a hotel/hostel for a longer period of time as you cannot complete necessary steps after arrival (bank account, registration etc.) without a permanent address.
In case of any questions related to online-studying options, please contact the head of your study program (regular programs only. Graduate School students, please check the latestFAQs).
faqs.Can you contact the embassy/immigration:
No.
We are aware that visa appointments are limited in some countries and there might be a waiting time of several months. However, the University cannot speed up the visa process. We do not have any influence on the waiting times or outcome of visa applications. The decision is made on a high political level, which we cannot influence in any direction.
faqs.Can I get a letter confirming that my ph: Physical Presence Letters are no longer required for visa application or entry to Germany. They willnotspeed up the visa process. Therefore, such letters will not be issued.
faqs.Can I get a letter confirming that I can:
Such a letter is not required as your admission letter confirms the latest starting date (in case there is such a date determined - otherwise you can arrive anytime during the semester).
Students facing entry/visa restrictions can contact the head of their study program to discuss options on starting their studies online. Students of our Graduate School, please check the latestFAQs.
faqs.Whom should I name as a reference in my: During your visa application process, you might be asked to give a reference person and/or contact details of the university. Please mention the person who issued your admission letter as a reference (admission office). In the admission letter, you can also find their contact details, including the address of the university and phone number.
faqs.Can I bring my family while I am studyin:
This depends on your individual situation. EU-citizen usually can bring their spouses/children without a visa. Non-EU citizen can also bring their spouse/under-age children when certain conditions are met, among others:
It is best to inform yourself at your local German embassy about the requirements for your individual situation. Moreover, you can have a look at the information providedhereandhere.
• Valid residence in Germany
• Separate visa applications/appointments for all accompanying family members
• Proof of sufficient financial resources for all accompanying family members
• Basic German skills(exceptions for some countries) of spouse
• Valid health insurance for all accompanying family members
• Proof of housing
faqs.Can I stay in Germany after I finish my: Yes, non-EU nationals are allowed to stay in Germany for a specific time after completing their studies successfully, even when no longer enrolled. For the period between graduating and finding a job, it is possible to extend your residence permit for 18 months. Once you have been accepted for a job, you will have to apply for a work visa. More information is availablehere.
faqs.When will I be officially enrolled?:
The official enrolment will be conductedonlinebefore the semester start (before October 1 or March 15).Enrollment is only possible if ...
... the complete fee mentioned in your admission letter has been paid
... all required documents have been submitted via Primuss
... you have taken out a valid health insurance, contacted a public German health insurance provider about your enrollment and the provider informed Hof University about your insurance status.
Once you have been enrolled, you will receive an automated email about this.
Enrollment & campus card
• ... the complete fee mentioned in your admission letter has been paid
• ... all required documents have been submitted via Primuss
• ... you have taken out a valid health insurance, contacted a public German health insurance provider about your enrollment and the provider informed Hof University about your insurance status.
faqs.When can I get my enrollment certificate: Your enrolment certificate will be available to download at the end of September (WS) or mid-March (SS) from Primuss student portal. This is only possible if you are enrolled and after you have received your student credentials.
faqs.What are the steps after my arrival? Whe: After your arrival, you must complete some administrative steps for living in Germany, such as city registration or opening a bank account/activating your blocked account. Also, you must arrange formalities for your studies, e.g. updating your address in Primuss portal or, at the semester start, organize your courses. All enrolled students can find information about the process after arrivalin our moodle portal here.
faqs.Where do I get my campus card after arri:
Please see here:Campus Card
Please note: If we have not received information about your health insurance status because you failed to contact a German statutory health insurance provider, you will not be enrolled and your campus card cannot be created.
